101.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'How bad it is that you equate us with dogs and donkeys! I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ praying while I was lying stretched out in front of him. When he wanted to prostrate, he nudged my leg, so I drew them in, and then he prostrated.'
101.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Alangkah buruknya kalian menyamakan kami dengan anjing dan keledai! Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ shalat sementara aku berbaring melintang di hadapannya. Ketika beliau hendak bersujud, beliau menyentuh kakiku, maka aku melipatnya, lalu beliau bersujud.'

102.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) regarding the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ after the final Isha prayer: she said it was nine rak'ahs standing, then two seated, and then two after the two adhan calls.
102.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) mengenai shalat Rasulullah ﷺ setelah shalat Isya yang terakhir: ia berkata, itu sembilan rakaat berdiri, kemudian dua sambil duduk, dan kemudian dua setelah dua kali adzan.

103.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite when entering the house: 'If the son of Adam had two valleys of wealth, he would seek a third. Nothing fills his mouth except dust, and Allah turns in forgiveness to whoever repents.'
103.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengucapkan ketika memasuki rumah: 'Jika anak Adam memiliki dua lembah harta, ia akan mencari yang ketiga. Tidak ada yang memenuhi mulutnya kecuali tanah, dan Allah menerima taubat siapa saja yang bertaubat.'

104.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The most detestable of men to Allah is the one who is most contentious and quarrelsome.'
104.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang yang paling dibenci Allah adalah orang yang paling suka bertengkar dan berselisih.'

105.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha and Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that Abu Bakr kissed the Prophet ﷺ after he had passed away.
105.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah dan Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Abu Bakar mencium Nabi ﷺ setelah beliau wafat.

106.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was told that Ibn Umar said the Prophet ﷺ performed Umrah in Rajab. She said: 'May Allah forgive Abu Abd al-Rahman. He has forgotten. The Prophet ﷺ did not perform Umrah in Rajab.' Ibn Umar could hear this and said neither yes nor no — he remained silent.
106.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia diberitahu bahwa Ibnu Umar berkata bahwa Nabi ﷺ melakukan Umrah pada bulan Rajab. Ia berkata: 'Semoga Allah mengampuni Abu Abdurrahman. Ia telah lupa. Nabi ﷺ tidak melakukan Umrah pada bulan Rajab.' Ibnu Umar dapat mendengar ini dan tidak mengatakan ya atau tidak — ia tetap diam.

107.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'He used to command me to wrap a garment around myself while I was menstruating, and then he would be intimate with me. I would also wash his head while he was in i'tikaf and I was menstruating.'
107.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Beliau biasa memerintahkanku untuk mengencangkan pakaianku sementara aku sedang haid, kemudian beliau bercumbu denganku. Aku juga biasa mencuci rambutnya sementara beliau beri'tikaf dan aku sedang haid.'

108.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Jibreel conveys his greetings to you.' She said: 'And upon him be peace and the mercy of Allah.'
108.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Jibril menyampaikan salam untukmu.' Ia berkata: 'Dan atasnya juga salam dan rahmat Allah.'

109.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked whether the Messenger of Allah ﷺ designated certain days for special worship. She said: 'No. His deeds were consistent. Which of you is capable of what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was capable of?'
109.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya apakah Rasulullah ﷺ menentukan hari-hari tertentu untuk ibadah khusus. Ia berkata: 'Tidak. Amalannya konsisten. Siapakah di antara kalian yang mampu melakukan apa yang mampu dilakukan oleh Rasulullah ﷺ?'

110.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Indeed the grave has a squeezing, and if anyone were to be saved from it, Sa'd ibn Mu'adh would have been saved.'
110.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya kubur memiliki tekanan, dan jika ada seseorang yang diselamatkan darinya, niscaya Sa'd bin Mu'adz yang diselamatkan.'

111.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever loves to meet Allah, Allah loves to meet him; and whoever hates to meet Allah, Allah hates to meet him. And death comes before meeting Allah Almighty.'
111.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang cinta bertemu Allah, Allah pun cinta bertemu dengannya; dan barangsiapa yang benci bertemu Allah, Allah pun benci bertemu dengannya. Dan kematian mendahului pertemuan dengan Allah Yang Maha Agung.'

112.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Among the nations before there were muhaddathun [inspired people]. If there is one such person in my nation, it is Umar.'
112.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Di antara umat-umat terdahulu terdapat muhaddatun [orang-orang yang diberi ilham]. Jika ada seseorang seperti itu di umatku, maka itu adalah Umar.'

113.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ kissed Uthman ibn Mazun after he had passed away, until I could see tears flowing down his face.'
113.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mencium Utsman bin Mazun setelah ia wafat, hingga aku melihat air mata mengalir di wajah beliau.'

114.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you becomes drowsy, let him lie down until sleep leaves him. For if he prays while drowsy, he may intend to seek forgiveness but end up cursing himself.'
114.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketika salah seorang dari kalian mengantuk, hendaklah ia berbaring hingga kantuk meninggalkannya. Karena jika ia shalat dalam keadaan mengantuk, ia mungkin berniat memohon ampunan tetapi justru mengutuk dirinya sendiri.'

115.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that when they arrived in Medina it was the most disease-ridden land of Allah, and Abu Bakr fell ill. So the Messenger of Allah ﷺ supplicated: 'O Allah, make Medina beloved to us as we love Mecca or even more so. Make it healthy and bless us in its mudd and sa'. Transfer its fever and place it in al-Juhfa.'
115.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ketika mereka tiba di Madinah, itu adalah tanah yang paling banyak wabahnya, dan Abu Bakar jatuh sakit. Maka Rasulullah ﷺ berdoa: 'Ya Allah, jadikanlah Madinah dicintai kami sebagaimana kami mencintai Mekah atau bahkan lebih dari itu. Jadikanlah ia sehat dan berkahilah kami dalam mudd dan sa'-nya. Pindahkanlah demamnya dan letakkan di al-Juhfah.'

116.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that some of the Prophet's companions said to him: 'O Messenger of Allah, we are not like you — Allah has forgiven all your past and future sins.' He would become angry, and the anger would be visible on his face, and then he would say: 'By Allah, I am the most knowledgeable of you about Allah and the most God-fearing of you at heart.'
116.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa beberapa sahabat Nabi berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Rasulullah, kami tidak sepertimu — Allah telah mengampuni semua dosa masa lalumu dan masa depanmu.' Beliau akan marah, dan kemarahan tampak di wajahnya, kemudian beliau berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku adalah yang paling berpengetahuan di antara kalian tentang Allah dan yang paling bertakwa di antara kalian di dalam hati.'

117.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that Umar said something to her, and she went back to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was eating and told him what Umar had said. Then revelation came to him, and when it lifted, he said: 'You have been given permission to go out for your needs.'
117.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Umar mengatakan sesuatu kepadanya, dan ia kembali kepada Rasulullah ﷺ sementara beliau sedang makan dan menceritakan apa yang dikatakan Umar. Kemudian wahyu turun kepadanya, dan ketika terangkat, beliau bersabda: 'Kalian telah diberi izin untuk keluar untuk keperluan kalian.'

118.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that a Bedouin came and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, do you kiss children? By Allah, we do not kiss them.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'What can I do if Allah has removed mercy from your heart?'
118.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa seorang Badui datang dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, apakah engkau mencium anak-anak? Demi Allah, kami tidak mencium mereka.' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apa yang bisa aku lakukan jika Allah telah mencabut rahmat dari hatimu?'

119.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Seek Laylat al-Qadr in the last ten nights of Ramadan.'
119.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Carilah Lailatul Qadar di sepuluh malam terakhir Ramadan.'

120.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The mattress of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was made of leather stuffed with palm fiber.'
120.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kasur Rasulullah ﷺ terbuat dari kulit yang diisi dengan sabut kelapa.'

121.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas was struck on the day of the Battle of al-Khandaq by a man from Quraysh called Hibban ibn al-Araka in the basilic vein, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ set up a tent for him in the mosque so he could visit him easily.
121.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas terkena pada hari Perang al-Khandaq oleh seorang pria dari Quraisy bernama Hibban bin al-Araka di urat besar, dan Rasulullah ﷺ mendirikan sebuah tenda untuknya di masjid agar mudah mengunjunginya.

122.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Banu Qurayzah surrendered to the judgment of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he delegated the decision to Sa'd. Sa'd said: 'I judge that the fighters be killed, the women and children taken captive, and their wealth divided.' Hisham said: My father told me that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'You have judged among them with the judgment of Allah Almighty.'
122.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Banu Qurayzah menyerah pada hukum Rasulullah ﷺ, dan beliau mendelegasikan keputusan kepada Sa'd. Sa'd berkata: 'Aku memutuskan bahwa para pejuang dibunuh, wanita dan anak-anak dijadikan tawanan, dan harta mereka dibagi.' Hisyam berkata: Ayahku memberitahuku bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Engkau telah memutuskan di antara mereka dengan keputusan Allah Yang Maha Agung.'

123.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I was present with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on an Eid day, and I looked from over his shoulder. He lowered his shoulder for me, and I watched the performers from over his shoulder until I had my fill, then I went away.'
123.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku hadir bersama Rasulullah ﷺ pada hari raya, dan aku memandang dari atas bahunya. Beliau merendahkan bahunya untukku, dan aku memandang para pemain dari atas bahunya hingga aku puas, kemudian aku pergi.'

124.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Were it not that your people had recently left disbelief, I would have demolished the Kaaba and rebuilt it on the foundation of Ibrahim, peace be upon him. For when Quraysh built it, they fell short, and I would have made a back door for it.'
124.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika bukan karena kaummu baru saja meninggalkan kekufuran, niscaya aku akan meruntuhkan Ka'bah dan membangunnya kembali di atas pondasi Ibrahim, semoga keselamatan atasnya. Karena ketika Quraisy membangunnya, mereka kurang sempurna, dan aku akan membuatkan pintu belakang untuknya.'

125.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I used to play with dolls, and my friends would come and play with me. When they saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, they would hide from him. And the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would bring them to me so they could play with me.'
125.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku biasa bermain dengan boneka-boneka, dan teman-temanku datang dan bermain bersamaku. Ketika mereka melihat Rasulullah ﷺ, mereka bersembunyi darinya. Dan Rasulullah ﷺ memasukkan mereka kepadaku agar mereka bisa bermain bersamaku.'

126.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she and her companions prayed without ablution when they had no water, and complained to the Prophet ﷺ. Allah revealed the verse of tayammum. Usayd ibn Hudayr said to Aisha: 'May Allah reward you well. By Allah, whenever something difficult befell you, Allah made it good for you and for the Muslims.'
126.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia dan para sahabatnya shalat tanpa wudhu ketika mereka tidak memiliki air, dan mengadu kepada Nabi ﷺ. Allah menurunkan ayat tayammum. Usaid bin Hudhair berkata kepada Aisyah: 'Semoga Allah membalasmu dengan kebaikan. Demi Allah, setiap kali sesuatu yang sulit menimpamu, Allah menjadikannya kebaikan bagimu dan bagi kaum Muslimin.'

127.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ was asked about the magic cast on him. He said: 'Labid ibn al-A'sam did it, using a comb and hair trimmings in a palm spathe of a male tree.' He was asked where it was. He said: 'In the well of Arwan.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went there with some companions, then returned and said: 'O Aisha, its water is like henna soak, and its palm trees are like devils' heads.' She said: 'O Messenger of Allah, why did you not burn it?' He said: 'As for me, Allah has cured me. I disliked stirring up evil among the people.' He then ordered it to be buried.
127.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ ditanya tentang sihir yang dilakukan padanya. Beliau bersabda: 'Labid bin al-A'sham melakukannya, menggunakan sisir dan potongan rambut dalam spatula kurma jantan.' Beliau ditanya di mana lokasinya. Beliau bersabda: 'Di sumur Arwan.' Rasulullah ﷺ pergi ke sana dengan beberapa sahabat, kemudian kembali dan bersabda: 'Wahai Aisyah, airnya seperti rendaman inai, dan pohon kurmanya seperti kepala setan.' Ia berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, mengapa engkau tidak membakarnya?' Beliau bersabda: 'Adapun aku, Allah telah menyembuhkanku. Aku tidak suka menimbulkan keburukan di antara orang-orang.' Kemudian beliau memerintahkan agar dikubur.

128.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to make these supplications: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the trial of the Fire and the punishment of the Fire; from the trial of the grave and the punishment of the grave; from the evil of the trial of wealth and the evil of the trial of poverty; and I seek refuge in You from the trial of the Masih al-Dajjal. O Allah, cleanse my sins with water of snow and hail, and purify my heart of sins as a white garment is purified.'
128.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membaca doa-doa ini: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari fitnah neraka dan azab neraka; dari fitnah kubur dan azab kubur; dari keburukan fitnah kekayaan dan keburukan fitnah kemiskinan; dan aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari fitnah Masih al-Dajjal. Ya Allah, bersihkanlah dosa-dosaku dengan air salju dan hujan es, dan sucikanlah hatiku dari dosa-dosa sebagaimana pakaian putih disucikan.'

129.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she disputed the statement that 'the deceased is punished for the weeping of the living.' She said: 'O Abu Abd al-Rahman, he only said that the family of the deceased weep over him, and he is punished for his own sins.'
129.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia membantah pernyataan bahwa 'orang yang meninggal disiksa karena tangisan orang yang hidup.' Ia berkata: 'Wahai Abu Abdurrahman, beliau hanya mengatakan bahwa keluarga orang yang meninggal menangisinya, dan ia disiksa karena dosanya sendiri.'

130.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ sat and prayed while behind him people were praying standing. He gestured to them to sit. When he finished the prayer, he said: 'The imam is appointed to be followed. When he bows, bow; when he rises, rise; and when he prays seated, pray seated.'
130.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ duduk dan shalat sementara di belakangnya orang-orang shalat dalam keadaan berdiri. Beliau memberi isyarat kepada mereka untuk duduk. Ketika selesai shalat, beliau bersabda: 'Imam ditetapkan untuk diikuti. Ketika ia ruku, rukuklah; ketika ia bangkit, bangkitlah; dan ketika ia shalat sambil duduk, shalatlah sambil duduk.'

131.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said to Ammar: 'You heard — or did you hear — the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "It is not lawful to shed the blood of a Muslim except for one of three things: one who commits adultery after being married, one who abandons Islam after embracing it, or one who kills a soul and is killed for it."'
131.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata kepada Ammar: 'Engkau mendengar — atau apakah engkau mendengar — Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Tidak halal menumpahkan darah seorang Muslim kecuali karena salah satu dari tiga hal: seseorang yang berzina setelah menikah, seseorang yang meninggalkan Islam setelah memeluknya, atau seseorang yang membunuh jiwa dan dibunuh karenanya."'

132.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ used to pray without placing anything on the ground as a barrier, except that I recall during a rainy day we laid a mat under him. I could see a tear in it from which water was seeping, yet he prayed on it.
132.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa shalat tanpa meletakkan apapun di atas tanah sebagai penghalang, kecuali yang aku ingat pada hari hujan kami menggelar tikar di bawahnya. Aku bisa melihat sobekan di dalamnya yang mengeluarkan air, namun beliau tetap shalat di atasnya.

133.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave her a camel from the sadaqah camels and said: 'O Aisha, adhere to God-consciousness and be gentle, for gentleness never enters into anything except that it beautifies it, and it is never removed from anything except that it mars it.'
133.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memberinya seekor unta dari unta-unta sedekah dan bersabda: 'Wahai Aisyah, bertakwalah kepada Allah dan berlaku lembutlah, karena kelembutan tidak masuk ke dalam sesuatu apapun kecuali memperindahnya, dan tidak dicabut dari sesuatu apapun kecuali merusaknya.'

134.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Breaking the bone of a believer when he is dead is like breaking it when he is alive.'
134.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Memecahkan tulang seorang mukmin ketika ia telah meninggal adalah seperti memecahkannya ketika ia masih hidup.'

135.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'Revelation used to come down upon the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on a cold morning, and his forehead would be drenched in sweat.'
135.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Wahyu biasa turun kepada Rasulullah ﷺ pada pagi yang dingin, dan dahinya dibasahi keringat.'

136.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I was never as jealous of any woman as I was of Khadija, though she had passed away three years before he married me, because I used to hear him mention her. His Lord commanded him to give her glad tidings of a palace in Paradise made of hollow pearls, and he used to slaughter sheep and send them as gifts to her friends.'
136.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah cemburu kepada wanita manapun sebagaimana aku cemburu kepada Khadijah, meskipun ia telah meninggal tiga tahun sebelum beliau menikahinya, karena aku sering mendengar beliau menyebutnya. Tuhannya memerintahkannya untuk memberi kabar gembira padanya tentang istana di surga yang terbuat dari mutiara berongga, dan beliau biasa menyembelih domba dan mengirimkannya sebagai hadiah kepada teman-temannya.'

137.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered Mecca on the day of the Conquest through Kada from the upper part of Mecca, and entered during Umrah through Kudaa.
137.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ masuk ke Mekah pada hari Penaklukan melalui Kada dari bagian atas Mekah, dan masuk saat Umrah melalui Kuda'a.

138.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I was startled one night and reached out my hand, which fell upon the Prophet's ﷺ feet, which were upright while he was in prostration saying: "I seek refuge in Your pleasure from Your wrath, in Your pardon from Your punishment, and I seek refuge in You from You. I cannot enumerate Your praises — You are as You have praised Yourself."'
138.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku terkejut suatu malam dan mengulurkan tanganku, yang jatuh pada kaki Nabi ﷺ, yang tegak lurus sementara beliau dalam keadaan bersujud dan mengucapkan: "Aku berlindung dengan ridha-Mu dari murka-Mu, dengan ampunan-Mu dari hukuman-Mu, dan aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari diri-Mu. Aku tidak dapat menghitung pujian-Mu — Engkau adalah sebagaimana Engkau memuji diri-Mu sendiri."'

139.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ was told that a man forbade women from going out to the mosques, and they did not obey him even on the third time. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Throw dust in their faces.' Aisha said: 'May Allah humble your nose! By Allah, you cannot do what he commanded, nor did you leave the Messenger of Allah ﷺ alone.'
139.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ diberitahu bahwa seorang pria melarang wanita pergi ke masjid-masjid, dan mereka tidak menaatinya bahkan pada kali ketiga. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Lemparkan tanah di wajah mereka.' Aisyah berkata: 'Semoga Allah merendahkan hidungmu! Demi Allah, kamu tidak bisa melakukan apa yang beliau perintahkan, dan kamu juga tidak membiarkan Rasulullah ﷺ sendirian.'

140.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to be intimate while fasting, but he would place a garment between himself and her private parts.
140.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa bercumbu sementara berpuasa, tetapi beliau meletakkan pakaian antara dirinya dan kemaluannya.

141.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whatever is below the ankle of the lower garment is in the Fire."'
141.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Bagian dari sarung yang berada di bawah mata kaki berada di neraka."'

142.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she described how the Prophet ﷺ forbade honey, and she cooperated with Hafsa and Sawda in this. She said: 'I told him: "The bees have fed on the urfa tree," and he declared honey forbidden for himself. It was then revealed: "O Prophet, why do you forbid what Allah has made lawful for you?"'
142.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia menggambarkan bagaimana Nabi ﷺ mengharamkan madu atas dirinya, dan ia bekerja sama dengan Hafsha dan Saudah dalam hal ini. Ia berkata: 'Aku memberitahunya: "Lebah-lebah telah memakan pohon urfa," dan beliau mengharamkan madu atas dirinya. Kemudian turunlah: "Wahai Nabi, mengapa engkau mengharamkan apa yang Allah halalkan bagimu?"'

143.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) regarding the incident of the false accusation, that she said: 'By Allah, I cannot find for myself and for you a better example than the father of Yusuf, peace be upon him — "So beautiful patience, and Allah is the one sought for help against what you describe." Then revelation came down to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ at that moment, and she could see the joy visible on his face as he wiped his forehead.'
143.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) mengenai peristiwa tuduhan palsu, bahwa ia berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku tidak dapat menemukan untuk diriku dan untuk kalian perumpamaan yang lebih baik daripada ayah Yusuf, semoga keselamatan atasnya — "Maka kesabaran yang indah, dan Allah-lah tempat meminta pertolongan atas apa yang kalian sifatkan." Kemudian wahyu turun kepada Rasulullah ﷺ saat itu juga, dan ia bisa melihat kegembiraan tampak di wajahnya ketika beliau mengusap dahinya.'

144.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'I know when you are pleased with me and when you are angry with me.' She said: 'How do you know that?' He said: 'When you are pleased with me, you say: "No, by the Lord of Muhammad." And when you are angry with me, you say: "No, by the Lord of Ibrahim."' She said: 'Indeed, by Allah, I only abandon your name.'
144.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku tahu ketika kamu senang kepadaku dan ketika kamu marah kepadaku.' Ia berkata: 'Bagaimana engkau tahu itu?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ketika kamu senang kepadaku, kamu berkata: "Tidak, demi Tuhan Muhammad." Dan ketika kamu marah kepadaku, kamu berkata: "Tidak, demi Tuhan Ibrahim."' Ia berkata: 'Memang, demi Allah, aku hanya meninggalkan namamu.'

145.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that when the companions said to the Prophet ﷺ: 'We are not like you — Allah has forgiven all your past and future sins,' he would become angry until the anger was visible on his face, then say: 'By Allah, I am the most knowledgeable of you about Allah and the most God-fearing of you at heart.'
145.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ketika para sahabat berkata kepada Nabi ﷺ: 'Kami tidak sepertimu — Allah telah mengampuni semua dosa masa lalumu dan masa depanmu,' beliau akan marah hingga kemarahan tampak di wajahnya, kemudian berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku adalah yang paling berpengetahuan di antara kalian tentang Allah dan yang paling bertakwa di antara kalian di dalam hati.'

146.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Day of Bu'ath was a day that Allah prepared for His Messenger ﷺ. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Medina when its leadership had been divided, its nobility killed, and its people inclined toward Allah and His Messenger in embracing Islam.'
146.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Hari Bu'ats adalah hari yang Allah siapkan untuk Rasul-Nya ﷺ. Rasulullah ﷺ tiba di Madinah ketika kepemimpinannya telah terpecah, kebangsawanannya telah terbunuh, dan penduduknya condong kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya dalam memeluk Islam.'

147.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'When my innocence was revealed, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood on the pulpit and called them [the accusers] together.'
147.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika kesucianku dinyatakan, Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri di atas mimbar dan memanggil mereka [para penuduh] bersama-sama.'

148.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Take upon yourselves only those deeds you are able to perform, for Allah does not tire until you tire.' Aisha said: 'The most beloved of deeds to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was the one performed most consistently, even if it were little. And whenever he performed a prayer, he kept it up.'
148.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Ambillah hanya amalan yang mampu kalian lakukan, karena Allah tidak bosan hingga kalian bosan.' Aisyah berkata: 'Amalan yang paling dicintai oleh Rasulullah ﷺ adalah yang paling konsisten dilakukan, meskipun sedikit. Dan setiap kali beliau melakukan suatu shalat, beliau terus melakukannya.'

149.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took her by the hand and showed her the moon as it rose, saying: 'Seek refuge in Allah from the evil of this darkening thing when it sets.'
149.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengambil tangannya dan menunjukkan kepadanya bulan ketika terbit, bersabda: 'Berlindunglah kepada Allah dari keburukan sesuatu yang menggelapkan ini ketika ia terbenam.'

150.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out for prayer while they were raising their voices, and he said: 'What is this?' She informed him of the dispute. He said: 'She spoke the truth.' From that day on, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would say at the end of every prayer: 'O Allah, Lord of Jibreel, Mika'il, and Israfil, protect me from the heat of the Fire and the punishment of the grave.'
150.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ keluar untuk shalat sementara mereka meninggikan suara-suara mereka, dan beliau berkata: 'Ada apa ini?' Ia memberitahu beliau tentang perselisihan. Beliau berkata: 'Ia berkata benar.' Sejak hari itu, Rasulullah ﷺ selalu mengucapkan di akhir setiap shalat: 'Ya Allah, Tuhan Jibril, Mikail, dan Israfil, lindungi aku dari panas neraka dan azab kubur.'
